idea.hook: Our Solar System is VAST... but imagine a single object WIDER than it!
facts: Our Sun's diameter is 1.4 million km.
Our entire planetary Solar System (out to Neptune's orbit) spans about 9 billion km.
TON 618, one of the largest known ultramassive black holes, has an event horizon diameter of 390 billion km.
idea.payoff: That means TON 618's event horizon is over 40 times wider than our ENTIRE planetary Solar System!
idea.title: Black Hole BIGGER Than Our Solar System?! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Our Solar System is HUGE. What's BIGGER?
scenes.visual: A stunning, wide shot of our Solar System, slowly panning or subtly zooming out to emphasize its vastness. Stars twinkle in the background.
A close-up, fiery image of the Sun. A tiny dot representing Earth appears for a brief moment next to it, then disappears as the text fades in.

scenes.text: Solar System (to Neptune): 9 billion km wide
scenes.visual: An animated infographic showing the Sun at the center, then planetary orbits (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) appearing outwards, highlighting the immense span.

scenes.text: TON 618 Black Hole: 390 billion km event horizon
scenes.visual: An abstract, stylized visualization of a black hole's event horizon, dark and ominous, with swirling accretion disk effects. The view slowly expands to suggest unimaginable size.

scenes.text: TON 618's event horizon is 40X wider than our ENTIRE Solar System!
scenes.visual: A dramatic side-by-side comparison. A small, complete representation of our Solar System (from Scene 3) shrinks and is placed next to a truly massive, almost overwhelming graphic of the TON 618 event horizon, emphasizing the scale difference.

youtube.title: Black Hole BIGGER Than Our Solar System?! 🤯🌌
